item.title: McConnell says he was "briefly unconscious," had pneumonia in health update
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell released a statement on his health on Sunday along with a photo of himself and his wife, Elaine Chao, after questions swirled about his condition.

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham dies from aortic dissection, medical examiner says
item.description: President Trump paid tribute to the late senator, who was reportedly scheduled to do an interview on Sunday.

item.title: At least 27 killed in Bangkok beer pub after fire broke out, authorities say
item.description: Footage shared online by first responders​ shows a huge blaze raging and plumes coming out of the front door of the Na Ladprao pub in the northern part of the Thai capital.

item.title: Keystone Pipeline operator agrees to pay $26.9 million over 2022 spill
item.description: A proposed settlement with the U.S. government would require the Keystone Pipeline system's operator to pay $26.9 million over a 2022 oil spill in Kansas.

item.title: Changing conditions cause expanded evacuation orders for Ferris Fire
item.description: Colorado officials expanded mandatory evacuation orders for residents near the Ferris Fire as conditions continued to change on Sunday.

item.title: Archaeologists uncover 3,000-year-old tomb near Egypt's Luxor
item.description: The burial site was identified as belonging to a man named Paser based on inscriptions.

item.title: Democratic Sen. Dick Durbin says Sen. Lindsey Graham was a "fair-minded person"
item.description: Democratic Sen. Dick Durbin of Illinois joins CBS News 24/7 to discuss his friendship and political clashes with Sen. Lindsey Graham, who died suddenly this weekend.

item.title: How Sen. Lindsey Graham's Senate seat will get filled
item.description: Longtime South Caroline Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham died at 71 after a "brief and sudden illness," his office said early Sunday morning. CBS News' Major Garrett breaks down how his Senate seat will be filled.

item.title: How Sen. Lindsey Graham's death impacts U.S. politics
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham's sudden death has shocked Washington, D.C. Political strategist Kevin Sheridan joins CBS News 24/7 to break down Graham's impact on U.S. politics.

item.title: What is the process for filling Lindsey Graham's vacancy in the Senate?
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham was running for reelection in November when he died suddenly on Saturday.

item.title: Transcript: Rep. Mike Turner on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," July 12, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of an interview with Republican Rep. Mike Turner of Ohio that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on July 12, 2026.

item.title: Ukraine's prime minister resigns; Zelenskyy shifts several government roles
item.description: Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko has stepped down as President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced fresh changes to Ukraine's government.

item.title: Here's what Lindsey Graham told Margaret Brennan about his Ukraine visit one day before his death
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham was in Kyiv, Ukraine, one day before his death. CBS News' Margaret Brennan recalls her final conversation with Sen. Graham on Friday, during which he touted progress on Russian oil sanctions.

item.title: Trump's monumental reimagining of Washington, D.C.
item.description: President Trump's demolition, construction and renovation efforts have triggered a firestorm of lawsuits, as critics seek to block his plans to remake our nation's capital.

item.title: Transcript: Michael Leiter on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," July 12, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of an interview with Israeli Ambassador to the U.S. Michael Leiter that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on July 12, 2026.

item.title: Transcript: Rahm Emanuel on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," July 12, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of an interview with former White House chief of staff and Chicago Mayor Rahm Emanuel that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on July 12, 2026.

item.title: South Carolina Rep. Nancy Mace on Sen. Lindsey Graham's death: "We're all in shock"
item.description: South Carolina Rep. Nancy Mace joined CBS News 24/7 to reflect on Sen. Lindsey Graham's political impact after his sudden death, saying she and other members of Congress are "in a state of shock."

item.title: Trump says he spoke with Sen. Lindsey Graham shortly before his sudden death
item.description: President Trump said Sunday that he spoke with Sen. Lindsey Graham shortly before the longtime Republican senator's death. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi has the latest.

item.title: Margaret Brennan: I spoke with Lindsey Graham a day before he died
item.description: Fierce Ukraine supporter Lindsey Graham passed away Saturday on the heels of his tenth trip to the warzone, and at a key moment for one of the Republican senator's proudest accomplishments.

item.title: Remembering Sen. Lindsey Graham's impact after his sudden death
item.description: Longtime Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham died Saturday night after a "brief and sudden illness," his office said. U.S. lawmakers and world leaders are paying tribute to the South Carolina native, and remembering his political and global impact.

item.title: 7/12: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," Sen. Tim Scott, Israeli Ambassador Michael Leiter, Rep. Mike Turner and Rahm Emanuel pay tribute to Sen. Lindsey Graham after his sudden death.

item.title: 7/12: Sunday Morning
item.description: Hosted by Tracy Smith. Featured: The only successful coup in U.S. history; Behind the scenes of "The Pitt"; Trump's monumental reimagining of Washington, D.C.; singer-songwriter Gracie Abrams; "Take Me Home, Country Roads"; and a Tuscany tradition: wine barrel races.

item.title: Training for a Tuscan tradition: The wine barrel race
item.description: Every August, the Italian town of Montepulciano becomes the backdrop of intense rivalries among neighborhood teams competing in the Bravìo delle Botti – the wine barrel race. Correspondent Seth Doane talks with locals training for the grueling competition: to roll 200-lb. wine barrels uphill through the medieval city's streets.

item.title: How white supremacists staged the only successful coup in U.S. history
item.description: In 1898, Wilmington, N.C., was prosperous and integrated. But white supremacists took back control of the city's multi-racial government at gunpoint, and killed scores of Black residents - a little-known story retold in Lauren Collins' "They Stole a City."

item.title: New Jersey works to destroy firefighting foam laced with cancer-linked PFAS
item.description: New Jersey is one of more than a dozen states that are working to collect, remove and destroy all of their aqueous film-forming foam.

item.title: Dave Portnoy on running "Barstool Sports" like "SNL"
item.description: Dave Portnoy founded "Barstool Sports" after quitting a sales job he hated and deciding to strike out on his own.

item.title: South African soccer player who competed in the World Cup dies at 25
item.description: Jayden Adams' death was confirmed by South Africa's minister of sport, arts and culture on Saturday.

item.title: New York Times journalists issued subpoenas over Air Force One reporting
item.description: The subpoenas were issued after the New York Times reported on alleged security concerns with the new Qatari-gifted Air Force One.

item.title: Notable Deaths in 2026
item.description: A look back at the esteemed personalities who've left us this year, who'd touched us with their innovation, creativity and humanity.

item.title: Summer music highlights of 2026
item.description: Summer is the time to enjoy live music, indoors and out. Scroll through our gallery of some of 2026's leading musical acts, featuring images by CBS News photojournalist Jake Barlow and photographers Ed Spinelli and Kirstine Walton.
